## Type of Change

Please delete options that are not relevant or check the applicable boxes.

- [ ] ✨ New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality)
- [ ] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)
- [ ] 💥 Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected)
- [ ] 📚 Documentation update
- [ ] 🧹 Refactor (non-breaking change that neither fixes a bug nor adds a feature)
- [ ] ⚙️ Build/CI/CD related changes
- [ ] 🧪 Test enhancement (adding missing tests, improving existing tests)
- [ ]  chore: Other changes (e.g., updating dependencies, repository maintenance)

## How Has This Been Tested?

Please describe the tests that you ran to verify your changes. Provide instructions so we can reproduce, if applicable.

- [ ] Unit tests added/updated for new/changed logic.
- [ ] Integration tests added/updated to cover interactions.
- [ ] Manual E2E testing performed (describe steps below, if any).

## Checklist for Contributor

- [ ] I have read the project's [**CONTRIBUTING.md**](./CONTRIBUTING.md) document.
- [ ] My code follows the style guidelines of this project (I have run `pnpm format` and `pnpm lint`).
- [ ] I have performed a self-review of my own code.
- [ ] I have commented my code, particularly in hard-to-understand areas.
- [ ] I have made corresponding changes to the documentation (if applicable).
- [ ] My changes generate no new warnings or errors.
- [ ] I have added tests that prove my fix is effective or that my feature works.
- [ ] New and existing unit tests pass locally with my changes.
- [ ] Any dependent changes have been merged and published in downstream modules (if applicable).
- [ ] I have added a [**Changeset**](https://github.com/changesets/changesets) if this change affects a published package (`pnpm changeset`). Provide a brief summary for the changelog.
